codeReview.summary: The range-anchor and suggestion-gating changes in commit 4b21b133 implement the requested beta comment extension behavior, but a later change on top of that feature introduced a blocking regression in summary rendering that reuses `review_runs.error_detail` for degradation summaries.

issues.description: `renderSummaryBody` now returns `run.ErrorDetail` whenever `run.ErrorCode == "degradation_mode"`, but the processor never persists the degradation summary into `review_runs.error_detail` (it clears the field in `UpdateReviewRunStatus` and stores the text only via `persistSummaryNoteFallback`). As a result, degraded runs fall back to the generic summary body instead of the explicit partial-coverage summary required by the large-MR/degradation flow. This later regression affects the reviewed beta comment-extension surface because `internal/writer/writer.go` was modified again after the feature commit.

summary: Reviewed the beta-comment-extensions feature by reading the mission context, AGENTS/services/library guidance, the feature entry in features.json, the worker transcript skeleton, the handoff, and the feature commit diff (4b21b133). The feature commit itself correctly adds GitLab `line_range` payload handling and keeps suggestion blocks gated by valid high-confidence patches. However, the current code in `internal/writer/writer.go` contains a later blocking regression at line 594: degradation summaries rely on `review_runs.error_detail`, but provider degradation handling never stores that text there, so partial-coverage summary notes are not rendered as intended in the live tree.
